title: Doc updates from QA pass: --colocate, memory decay, multi-machine recipe, prefix contract
body:
# Goal

Several findings from the 2026-06-24 QA pass are pure
documentation gaps that don't need code changes. Batch them
into one edit pass over `CLAUDE.md` and `docs/quickstart.md`.

# Items

## 1. Quickstart over-specifies `--colocate`

`docs/quickstart.md` step 1 says:

```
jj git init --colocate
```

On jj 0.40 (and probably earlier), plain `jj git init` also
produces `.git/` + `.jj/` and works fine with `jjf`. The
`--colocate` flag isn't required.

**Fix:** drop the flag or note that on jj 0.40+ it's the
default. (Picking what to do depends on jj's stability story
for the default — investigate before changing.)

## 2. Memories don't auto-decay

The jjforge planner carries a memory about
`JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` (retired in v3 — confirmed via
`52e5c49`). Stale memories drift like comments do.

**Fix:** add a short paragraph to CLAUDE.md (in the memory
section) noting that memories need active pruning when the
underlying invariant changes, and mention `jjf forget <key>`
in the quickstart memory section.

## 3. Multi-machine setup recipe (depends on F-001 fix)

Once F-001 is fixed, the quickstart should grow a section
"Joining an existing project" with the exact recipe (clone,
the auto-config, first pull, first push). The current
quickstart only covers `mkdir my-project`.

## 4. Prefix-id contract (depends on F-008 outcome)

If F-008 ends up "docs are wrong, prefixes never worked,"
CLAUDE.md's "often 4 characters is enough" claim needs
removal. If F-008 restores the feature, the contract stays.

## 5. No `jjf dep ls` (audit gap)

CLAUDE.md's "Filters jjforge doesn't yet ship" list already
mentions wanting a dep audit verb. F-009 raises this from
nice-to-have to "we'll hit the cycle-detection diagnostic gap
without it." Update the wishlist note to flag the priority.

# Confidence

Items 1, 2, 5 are doc-only and can land immediately. Items 3
and 4 are gated on F-001 and F-008 respectively.
